title: fix: require approval for production secret changes
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds a mandatory, explicit, scoped approval gate before any secret edit or secret-change PR
- Defines the exact approval sentence and prevents general task approval from counting
- Adds the same checkpoint to the model-management skill and its SOPS procedure
- Requires secret changes to remain separate from model and feature PRs
- Does not modify workflows, environments, or credential values

gist.headline: Secrets need a second nod
gist.blurb: The hive now pauses before touching production secrets. A specific human sentence is required; vague approval pollen does not count.
gist.summary: Added an explicit, scoped approval checkpoint for every production secret edit or secret-change PR in AGENTS.md, model-management guidance, and the SOPS procedure, keeping secret work separate from model and feature changes.
gist.impact: Contributors now get a clear mandatory approval step before production-secret changes, making sensitive edits easier to review independently.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Polli as a careful keeper of a small locked seed vault labeled "SECRETS," holding a checklist with one clearly stamped approval line while a friendly CRT monitor robot offers a separate sealed envelope. Use garden plants and a fence to show that secret changes stay in their own carefully tended plot.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polli, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink cheek blush and light blue wings, wearing a tiny safety helmet and holding a clipboard beside a small locked seed vault labeled "SECRETS" in chunky pixel text; a friendly CRT monitor robot presents a single stamped "APPROVED" note, while code-plants grow in a separate fenced garden bed. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, with Polli small-to-medium in the scene and subtle CRT glow.
